name: GitHub Actions
description: GitHub's integrated CI/CD platform built directly into the GitHub repository experience. Workflows are defined in YAML and triggered by GitHub events. Marketplace offers thousands of reusable actions.
notable_features: Deep GitHub integration with automatic event triggers
Reusable Actions marketplace with 20,000+ actions
Matrix builds for parallel multi-version testing
Environments with manual approval gates and secrets
OIDC-based keyless cloud authentication
Composite actions and reusable workflows

name: GitLab CI/CD
description: Fully integrated CI/CD solution built into GitLab. Tightly coupled with GitLab's source control, issue tracking, and container registry. Supports multi-project pipelines and Auto DevOps.
notable_features: Tightly integrated with GitLab SCM, registry, and security scanning
Auto DevOps for zero-config deployments to Kubernetes
Multi-project and parent-child pipelines
Built-in container registry and package registry
DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) job dependencies
Review Apps for ephemeral deployment previews

name: Jenkins
description: The most widely deployed open-source automation server. Highly extensible via 1,800+ plugins. Supports both declarative and scripted Pipeline DSL. Requires self-hosting and operational overhead.
notable_features: 1,800+ community plugins for virtually any integration
Declarative and Scripted Pipeline DSL (Groovy)
Jenkins X for cloud-native Kubernetes CI/CD
Blue Ocean modern UI for pipeline visualization
Master/agent distributed build architecture
On-premises with full data sovereignty

name: CircleCI
description: A cloud-native CI/CD platform known for fast build times, Docker-first workflows, and a flexible orb ecosystem. Supports parallelism and test splitting to optimize pipeline speed.
notable_features: Orbs: reusable, shareable configuration packages
Intelligent test splitting for parallel job execution
Resource classes with GPU, Arm, and large-memory options
Docker Layer Caching (DLC) for faster builds
Pipelines with fan-in/fan-out workflow support
Self-hosted runners for on-premises or custom environments

name: Travis CI
description: One of the first hosted CI services, popular in the open-source community. Simple YAML configuration and broad language support. Legacy platform that has declined in usage after pricing changes in 2020.
notable_features: Simple, minimal YAML configuration to get started
Wide language and runtime support out of the box
Build matrix for multi-environment testing
GitHub integration with automatic PR status checks
macOS and Linux build environments available
Historical open-source CI pioneer

name: Drone CI
description: A container-native CI/CD platform where every pipeline step runs in an isolated Docker container. Lightweight and easy to deploy. Forked as Woodpecker CI after licensing changes. Harness-owned.
notable_features: Every pipeline step runs in its own container
Plugin system using purpose-built Docker images
Native support for multiple SCM providers
Lightweight server with low resource requirements
Supports multiple runner types (Docker, Kubernetes, SSH, Exec)
Trusted and secure secret injection per repository

name: Buildkite
description: A hybrid CI/CD platform where the orchestration layer is cloud-hosted but all build agents run on your own infrastructure. Excellent for large engineering organizations with high build volumes and custom hardware needs.
notable_features: Agents run on your own infrastructure (any OS, GPU, bare metal)
Dynamic pipelines generated at runtime from scripts
Parallel matrix builds with automatic test splitting
Bazel and monorepo build optimization support
Audit logs, SSO, and enterprise compliance features
Plugin system with open-source community plugins

name: TeamCity
description: JetBrains' enterprise-grade CI/CD server with deep IDE integrations, powerful build chain features, and configuration as code via Kotlin DSL. Available as cloud (TeamCity Cloud) or on-premises.
notable_features: Kotlin DSL for version-controlled build configuration
Deep JetBrains IDE integration (IntelliJ IDEA, etc.)
Build chains for complex multi-project pipelines
Pre-tested (delayed) commits to keep the main branch green
Comprehensive build history and test flakiness detection
Personal builds for developer branch validation

name: Azure Pipelines
description: Microsoft's CI/CD service, part of Azure DevOps. Supports any language, platform, and cloud. Integrates deeply with GitHub, Azure services, and Microsoft tooling. Free for open-source projects.
notable_features: Cross-platform (Windows, Linux, macOS) hosted agents
Deep Azure and Microsoft ecosystem integration
Deployment environments with approval gates and audit history
YAML pipelines with templates and extends for reuse
Unlimited free parallel jobs for public open-source projects
Integrated artifact feeds and test reporting

name: Bitbucket Pipelines
description: Atlassian's integrated CI/CD solution for Bitbucket Cloud repositories. Docker-based builds with tight integration into Jira, Confluence, and the Atlassian ecosystem.
notable_features: Native Bitbucket and Jira integration
Deployment tracking with Jira issue linking
Pipes: curated Docker-based tasks for common operations
Branch-specific and pull-request pipelines
Parallel steps for faster builds
Built-in deployment environments (test, staging, production)

name: Concourse CI
description: An open-source, container-native CI/CD system built around the concepts of tasks, jobs, and resources. Designed for correctness and reproducibility — every task runs in a container with no implicit state.
notable_features: Stateless, reproducible builds with no implicit shared state
Resource abstraction for any external artifact (git, S3, registry, etc.)
Tasks are plain containers, shareable and testable locally via 'fly execute'
Fly CLI for full pipeline management from the command line
Visual pipeline graph in the web UI
Designed for large-scale, complex pipelines (CF Foundation origin)

name: Tekton
description: A Kubernetes-native CI/CD framework from the CD Foundation. Defines pipelines as Kubernetes Custom Resources (Tasks, Pipelines, PipelineRuns). The engine underlying OpenShift Pipelines and other enterprise products.
notable_features: Kubernetes-native CRDs for Tasks, Pipelines, and Triggers
Runs natively on any Kubernetes cluster
Tekton Hub for sharing reusable Tasks and Pipelines
Tekton Chains for supply chain security (SLSA provenance)
Highly extensible and modular component architecture
Underlying engine for Red Hat OpenShift Pipelines

name: Woodpecker CI
description: A community fork of Drone CI created after Harness changed Drone's license. Maintains the original open-source spirit with Docker-native pipelines. Supports Gitea, GitHub, GitLab, Forgejo, and Bitbucket.
notable_features: 100% open-source community-driven Drone CI fork
Docker-native: every step runs in a container
Multi-platform agent support (Linux, Windows, macOS)
Supports multiple SCM providers including Gitea and Forgejo
Simple YAML configuration with plugin ecosystem
Secrets management per repository or globally
